title: Xtra Chewy Ukrainian Meat Dumplings
description: Salty and chewy Ukrainian dish featuring mixed peel and a mirepoix base, this kosher friendly recipe is perfect for adventurous eaters. The combination of ground meats and spices creates a unique flavor profile.
instructions: In a large bowl, combine the ground beef, ground pork, mixed peel, onion, carrot, celery, egg, salt, and black pepper, and mix until just combined.
Gradually add the flour to the meat mixture and mix until a dough forms.
Knead the dough on a floured surface for 5 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Divide the dough into 20 equal pieces and roll each piece into a ball.
Flatten each ball into a disk shape using a rolling pin or your hands.
Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil and gently add the dumplings to the pot.
Reduce the heat to a simmer and cook the dumplings for 10 minutes, or until they float to the surface.
Remove the dumplings from the water with a slotted spoon and serve hot.
Traditionally, the dumplings are served with a side of sour cream or fried onions.
Cook pork to an internal temperature of 63°C / 145°F and let it rest for 3 minutes before serving.
Cook ground beef to an internal temperature of 71°C / 160°F (no longer pink in the middle) before serving.
